message:
tests(ft-e34d9): fragmented-read recovery preserves payload byte order

Adds a new regression test and e2e harness coverage for the mux
migration read-recovery path.

Scenario:
1. Seed a mock unix stream with a 36-byte payload
   ("fragmented-recovery-keeps-byte-order").
2. Wrap the reader in a SimulatedNetwork with read_error_rate=1.0
   and confirm the first read surfaces an error AND leaves the
   buffered bytes intact (bytes_read == 0, errors_injected == 1).
3. Re-wrap the SAME underlying stream in a fresh SimulatedNetwork
   with max_read_bytes=Some(7) to force fragmented reads of the
   recovery payload. Drain it in 7-byte chunks and assert the
   reassembled Vec<u8> exactly matches the original payload.

Why this matters:
A previous regression (ft-e34d9) left the mux in a state where a
transient read fault could consume bytes from the shared buffer
before the retry path ran, corrupting subsequent fragmented reads.
The existing `simulated_network_read_error_then_recovery_preserves_buffered_data`
test caught the buffered-byte loss but not the byte-order invariant
under fragmentation - which is the harder, more subtle bug since it
only manifests when max_read_bytes < payload_len.

Also updates the tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_5_2_mux_migration_completion.sh
structural + rch_phase blocks so CI both (a) grep-asserts the test
exists before running anything (catches a regression where the test
is accidentally deleted) and (b) runs it through the vendored
asupersync runtime in isolated cargo test invocation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -179,6 +179,12 @@ else
   record_structural_fail "read_recovery_test_presence" "missing" "E_READ_RECOVERY" "${STRUCTURAL_FILE}"
 fi
 
+if rg -q 'simulated_network_read_error_then_fragmented_recovery_preserves_payload_order' "${TEST_FILE}"; then
+  record_structural_pass "fragmented_read_recovery_test_presence" "present" "${STRUCTURAL_FILE}"
+else
+  record_structural_fail "fragmented_read_recovery_test_presence" "missing" "E_FRAGMENTED_READ_RECOVERY" "${STRUCTURAL_FILE}"
+fi
+
 if rg -q 'pool_timeout_cascade_then_recovery_restores_capacity' "${TEST_FILE}"; then
   record_structural_pass "timeout_recovery_test_presence" "present" "${STRUCTURAL_FILE}"
 else
@@ -232,6 +238,11 @@ run_rch_phase \
   "cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored simulated_network_read_error_then_recovery_preserves_buffered_data -- --test-threads=1" \
   test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored simulated_network_read_error_then_recovery_preserves_buffered_data -- --test-threads=1
 
+run_rch_phase \
+  "fragmented_read_recovery_targeted" \
+  "cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored simulated_network_read_error_then_fragmented_recovery_preserves_payload_order -- --test-threads=1" \
+  test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored simulated_network_read_error_then_fragmented_recovery_preserves_payload_order -- --test-threads=1
+
 run_rch_phase \
   "timeout_recovery_targeted" \
   "cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_timeout_cascade_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1" \
